report_name: Evolving NightSpire Ransomware Uses Fortinet Flaw and Aggressive Tactics in Double-Extortion Campaigns

threat_actors.name: NightSpire
threat_actors.description: An opportunistic, financially motivated ransomware group that emerged in February 2025. It operates a double-extortion model, using a dedicated data leak site and aggressive, short ransom deadlines. The group has shown a mix of sophisticated tooling and poor operational security.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through double-extortion ransomware campaigns.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The NightSpire group exploited CVE-2024-55591, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in public-facing Fortinet FortiOS devices, to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Brute Force
attack_patterns.description: The group utilized RDP brute-force attacks as one of its initial access methods.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The group utilized phishing campaigns as one of its initial access methods.

attack_patterns.name: System Network Connections Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Used during the internal reconnaissance phase after initial access to discover network connections.

attack_patterns.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Attackers moved tools like PsExec across the network to facilitate lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: Used for internal reconnaissance after initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The group exfiltrated sensitive data using legitimate tools like WinSCP and MEGAcmd to cloud storage services before deploying ransomware.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The NightSpire Go-based ransomware payload was deployed to encrypt files on victim systems, appending the .nspire extension.

malware.name: NightSpire Ransomware
malware.description: A Go-based ransomware payload used by the NightSpire group. It encrypts files and appends the .nspire extension. Being Go-based allows for easier cross-compilation for Windows and Linux targets.

tools.name: PowerShell
tools.description: Used by NightSpire operators for internal reconnaissance.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Used by NightSpire operators for lateral movement across the victim network.

tools.name: WinSCP
tools.description: Used by NightSpire operators for data exfiltration prior to ransomware deployment.

tools.name: MEGAcmd
tools.description: Used by NightSpire operators for data exfiltration to the MEGA cloud storage service.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2024-55591
vulnerabilities.description: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Fortinet's FortiOS, exploited by the NightSpire group for initial access.

infrastructure.name: NightSpire Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dedicated data leak site (DLS) used by the NightSpire group to publish stolen victim data as part of their double-extortion tactics.

indicators.name: NightSpire Ransomware File Creation (.nspire)
indicators.pattern:
title: NightSpire Ransomware File Creation (.nspire)
id: 0c8b3a1d-5e7f-4b9a-8c1d-2e3f4a5b6c7d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with the .nspire extension, which is indicative of NightSpire ransomware activity.
references:
  - https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/05/01/nightspire-wannabe-warlords-in-ransomwares-shadow-realm/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.nspire'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the mass creation of files with the .nspire extension, a key indicator of a NightSpire ransomware attack.

indicators.name: NightSpire Abused Tool Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: NightSpire Abused Tool Execution
id: 1d9c8b3a-7f5e-9a4b-1d8c-f2e3a4b5c6d7
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of legitimate tools (WinSCP, MEGAcmd, PsExec) known to be abused by the NightSpire ransomware group for lateral movement and data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://blog.barracuda.com/2026/05/01/nightspire-wannabe-warlords-in-ransomwares-shadow-realm/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-02
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.t1567.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\\WinSCP.exe'
      - '\\MEGAcmd.exe'
      - '\\PsExec.exe'
      - '\\Psexesvc.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of tools such as WinSCP, MEGAcmd, and PsExec, which are used by NightSpire for exfiltration and lateral movement.
